title: Greenhouse Zone Partition
strategies: one giant zone: assign every cell the same label -- zero walls, full mismatch; reproduces the evaluator's weak one-zone reference and scores ~0.1

global temperature bucketing: split the observed range into B equal bands and label each cell by its band; big mismatch drop but ignores geometry, so noisy neighbours create many walls and B is never tuned

agglomerative region merging: start one-cell-per-zone and repeatedly merge the adjacent zone pair whose merge most reduces total cost (mismatch increase minus removed-wall savings) until no improving merge remains -- directly optimizes the objective

seeded local search / metaheuristics: from a merged or band-segmented partition, relocate boundary cells or run perturb-and-remerge restarts to squeeze out remaining wall/mismatch trade-offs within the op budget
